title: update blocked-diagnostic + resolution loop — story-driven
description: Bring pi-context update's blocked outcome from an opaque dead-end to a diagnosed, resolvable state, and make the dry-run predict the live outcome. The work container for the update-arc gap set: FGAP-066 (dry/live outcome parity), FGAP-077 (per-item validation diagnostic), FGAP-030+FGAP-078 (check-status exposed as a CLI op + version-gap reporting), FGAP-079 (catalog schema locally diffable), FGAP-080 (pending-blocked state + resolve-blocked / update --continue), FGAP-081 (in-file failure markers, object-store recoverable). INVARIANT (every task preserves it): the per-schema byte-exact rollback and per-component application (DEC-0018) are preserved; no data loss on any blocked path; the only blocked-path write is the explicitly-declared marker-mode of FGAP-081, with the pre-marker block recoverable from the object store; in-pi op behavior changes only where a gap names it. The feature's success criteria are the user stories it advances — STORY-001..014, filed verbatim from the operator's in-pi reports — not embedded criteria text.
motivation: An operator hit blocked on a live update the dry-run had predicted safe, with no diagnostic naming the failing item/field and no resolution path — the inciting incident the 14 user stories articulate. The standard is best-of-breed: diagnose, fix, retry, with the dry-run telling the truth.
